question: Describe the configuration of the steam generators within the upper reactor pressure vessel (RPV).

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of steel and nickel-based alloys used, their protective claddings, and relevant material standards to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various internal systems and ensuring the containment of fission products. Materials for the RPV and its components are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and durability throughout the plant’s design life.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in nuclear facility applications.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It also outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation between primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and components, and is constructed and certified according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity. The materials used for the RPV are carefully selected to maintain safety and performance throughout the plant's design life.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It emphasizes compliance with specific codes for packaging, handling, and quality assurance, as well as maintaining a controlled environment during shipment to protect the reactor vessel's integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and provide heat transfer from the reactor to generate steam. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and internal components that separate the primary and secondary sides, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the steam generators.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the information for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

edges.description: Section B provides a general overview stating that the steam generators are contained within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section A details the configuration and materials of the upper RPV but does not specifically address the steam generators, thus Section A specifies details for the general description in Section B.

Section A describes the physical configuration and materials of the pressurizer components within the RPV upper shell, while Section B lists references and standards related to the integrity and material reliability of the reactor coolant boundary, which includes the RPV and its components.

Section B provides a general overview of the components within the RPV, including the steam generators, while Section A specifies details about the pressurizer configuration within the upper RPV but does not mention the steam generators.

Section B provides a general description that the steam generators are contained within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section A details the configuration of other components within the RPV but does not mention the steam generators, thus Section A specifies details for the broader context given in Section B.

Section A specifies the components and materials of the RPV upper shell and head, which are relevant to the configuration of the steam generators, while Section B discusses the inspection and handling requirements for the RPV as a whole, referencing the same components but focusing on their treatment rather than configuration.

Section A provides a general overview that the steam generators are contained within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies the detailed configuration by listing the steam generators among other components located inside the RPV.
Section A and Section B are identical excerpts that both state the steam generators are contained within the reactor pressure vessel, directly addressing the configuration in the analysis question.

Section A provides references that support the technical and regulatory basis for the design details described in Section B regarding the configuration of steam generators within the upper reactor pressure vessel.

Section A provides references and standards that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for the reactor coolant boundary, while Section B describes the physical configuration of the steam generators within the upper reactor pressure vessel, connecting the referenced standards to the actual design.

Section A and Section B are related because both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda, indicating that Section B's requirements for RPV shipment and installation are governed by the quality assurance standards cited in Section A.

Section A specifies the components and subsystems located within the upper reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor vessel and confirms that the steam generators are contained within the RPV.

Section A provides a general description of the components housed within the RPV, including the steam generators, while Section B specifies detailed information about the pressurizer configuration and materials within the RPV upper shell.

Both Section A and Section B state that the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) contains two steam generators as part of the integrated NuScale Power Module configuration, connecting them through their description of the RPV's internal components.

Section A is the preface of the document, while Section B is a reference section that points to specific technical details within the same document, indicating that Section A introduces the document that Section B references for detailed component descriptions.

Section B provides a detailed description of the configuration of the steam generators within the upper reactor pressure vessel, which is the specific information requested by the analysis question, whereas Section A is only a preface and does not address the configuration.

Section A is the preface of the chapter and does not provide technical details, while Section B lists references cited in the chapter, connecting them structurally as the preface introduces the document that will use the references listed in Section B.
